summary: Stunning binaural audience recording captures a classic Hartford show with soaring Truckin' jams, China/Rider, Weather Report Suite and Eyes of the World, despite minor hiss and a weaker Phil‑Ned segment.
review: The performance is praised for its epic Truckin > Wharf Rat stretch, fiery China Cat Sunflower/Rider, Let It Grow and the rare Weather Report Suite, all delivered with great energy and superb improvisation. The dummy‑head audience tape provides an immersive stereo field with excellent separation, though a faint hiss, slight muddiness and some distortion during the Phil & Ned (Seastones) passage are noted.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (average 4.5), strong praise for musical highlights and generally good to excellent recording quality; only one mixed comment about a specific segment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Binaural/dummy‑head stereo gives immersive space and great separation; faint hiss and slight muddiness noted, occasional distortion on the Phil & Ned (Seastones) portion.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower / I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set builds from solid opening numbers into a marathon Truckin' jam and culminates with expansive improvisations on Weather Report Suite, though the middle Seastones segment drags for some listeners.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work described as “wall of sound” with each note distinct, especially on Eyes of the World.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s contribution in the Phil & Ned (Seastones) sequence was called boring and distorted by one reviewer.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocals on El Paso were praised as nailed.
